title: Family state rows
description: Schema for rows in families/<family_id>/family_states.csv. Array-like fields are stored as JSON-encoded strings in the CSV export.

year.description: Milestone year represented by this state-year row. Phase 1 expects 2025, 2030, 2035, 2040, 2045, and 2050.

state_id.description: Stable state-family identifier repeated across milestone years. In this Phase 1 export it is unique within each (year, region) slice.

state_label.description: Human-readable state name shown in the explorer and supporting tables.

state_description.description: Plain-language description of what the state represents and how it differs from alternative states.

output_cost_per_unit.description: Real cost per output unit, usually in 2024 AUD, following the package's non-commodity conversion-cost convention unless otherwise noted. Use null or a blank CSV cell only when no defensible value is available.

cost_basis_year.description: Base year used to normalize output_cost_per_unit into real terms. Keep aligned with the currency label.

currency.description: Currency or price-basis label for output_cost_per_unit, such as AUD_2024.

cost_components_summary.description: Short note describing which cost components are included in or excluded from output_cost_per_unit.

input_commodities.description: JSON-encoded array of input commodity identifiers stored in one CSV cell. Keep the item order aligned one-for-one with input_coefficients and input_units.

input_coefficients.description: JSON-encoded array of per-unit input coefficients stored in one CSV cell. Keep the item order aligned one-for-one with input_commodities and input_units.

input_units.description: JSON-encoded array of coefficient units stored in one CSV cell. Keep the item order aligned one-for-one with input_commodities and input_coefficients.

input_basis_notes.description: Notes on how the input coefficients were derived, normalized, or interpreted.

energy_emissions_by_pollutant.description: JSON-encoded array of pollutant/value objects stored in one CSV cell, for example [{"pollutant":"CO2e","value":0.1}]. In end-use sectors this usually represents direct on-site energy emissions only.

process_emissions_by_pollutant.description: JSON-encoded array of pollutant/value objects stored in one CSV cell, for example [{"pollutant":"CO2e","value":0.1}]. Negative values denote removals or net negative process effects.

emissions_units.description: Unit label applied to the emissions values, such as tCO2e per output unit.

emissions_boundary_notes.description: Boundary note explaining what emissions are included, excluded, or shifted to another sector.

max_share.description: Optional upper bound on the share of total service or output demand this state may supply in the given year. Leave null or a blank CSV cell when no share cap is set.

max_activity.description: Optional absolute upper bound on activity or output in the given year. Leave null or a blank CSV cell when no activity cap is set.

min_share.description: Optional lower bound on the share of total service or output demand this state must supply in the given year. Leave null or a blank CSV cell when no minimum share is required.

rollout_limit_notes.description: Narrative explanation for any max_share, max_activity, or min_share limit.

availability_conditions.description: Text description of conditions that must hold for the state to be available or credible.

source_ids.description: JSON-encoded array of source IDs stored in one CSV cell. IDs should resolve to entries in source_ledger.csv when that companion ledger is supplied.

evidence_summary.description: Short summary of the evidence base supporting the row values.

derivation_method.description: Brief description of how the row was synthesized, scaled, interpolated, or calibrated from sources.

assumption_ids.description: JSON-encoded array of assumption IDs stored in one CSV cell. IDs should resolve to entries in assumptions_ledger.csv when that companion ledger is supplied.

confidence_rating.description: Qualitative confidence class for the row. Use one of High, Medium, Low, or Exploratory.

review_notes.description: Reviewer caveats, unresolved questions, or quality flags that should stay visible to downstream users.

candidate_expansion_pathway.description: Short note on how this reduced-form state could expand into a more explicit future model.

times_or_vedalang_mapping_notes.description: Notes on possible TIMES or VedaLang mapping, process-chain interpretation, or future model integration.

would_expand_to_explicit_capacity?.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this state would likely expand into an explicit capacity-style representation in a future model.

would_expand_to_process_chain?.description: Boolean flag indicating whether this state would likely expand into a more explicit process-chain representation in a future model.

family_id.description: Canonical family identifier. Must match the folder name and the entry in shared/families.csv.
